subject: URGENT: Production Environment Outage
body:
Urgent notification:

Starting at 20:15 tonight, the production environment is experiencing widespread service failures.

Initial symptoms:
1. Multiple API endpoints are timing out (>5 seconds)
2. Users are reporting that pages will not load
3. Monitoring system has been continuously triggering alerts

Current impact scope:
- Payment service is unavailable
- Order submissions are failing
- Background task queue has a large backlog

All relevant teams please investigate immediately. The DBA team has begun looking into database-related issues.

DevOps Team

subject: Re: Production Outage - Database Analysis
body:
Hi all,

I checked the database slow query log and found that the crm_data_sync process launched a new SQL query after starting at 20:00. This query is not using any index and is performing a full table scan. This SQL was introduced by the CRM sync logic change deployed last Friday.

Details:
- Process name: crm_data_sync
- Start time: 20:00 (triggered by scheduled job)
- Problematic SQL: SELECT * FROM customers JOIN orders ON customers.id = orders.customer_id WHERE sync_status = 'pending'
- This query scanned approximately 12 million rows
- The process was holding over 300 database connections
- The connection pool was completely exhausted (200/200); other services could not acquire new connections

I manually killed this process at 21:30 and suspended the corresponding scheduled job. Database connections are recovering.

Next steps needed:
1. Add an index on the sync_status field
2. Migrate CRM sync queries to a read replica
3. Limit the maximum number of connections per process

DBA Wang

subject: Last Friday's Deployment Contents Confirmation
body:
Hi everyone,

Regarding last night's outage, I have compiled the contents of last Friday's (February 27th) deployment:

Deployment change list:
1. CRM customer data sync optimization (modified sync query logic) -- Development team responsible
2. Order search functionality improvement -- Passed load testing
3. Payment results page optimization -- Frontend change, no backend impact

Code review records show:
- The CRM sync SQL change did not undergo DBA performance review
- During the review meeting, the DBA suggested additional performance testing, but it was not adopted due to time pressure
- The reviewer for that change was the development lead; the DBA review step was skipped

I suggest we discuss deployment process improvements during the postmortem.

Project Manager
